information: LeWM-VC flat bitrate root cause: sigma_floor=0.05 in laplace_likelihood_discrete created hard floor on negative log-likelihood. At step size 2/255, max PMF ~0.075 -> 3.74 bits/latent. With 192-channel latent compressed 16x, min bitrate = 3.74 / (3 * 16) * 192 = 0.93 bpp. Model literally could not go lower regardless of lambda. Fix: sigma_floor reduced to 0.003, max=5.0 clamp removed, encoder LR increased from 1e-5 to 5e-5. Fix requires FULL RETRAINING from Phase 0 checkpoint -- old checkpoints are stale because model learned to operate within old clamp.

GMM code review completed 2026-04-25. Two bugs found: (1) Negative bitrate in first gmm_lambda_sweep.py (rate formula mismatch with training) - fixed in v2. (2) Scale floor too small (1e-6) in GMMEntropyModel - bumped to 1e-5. Flat bitrate in GMM sweep (0.6735 bpp across all lambda) was NOT a GMM bug - same root cause as Laplace model: single-lambda training produces a single operating point. Fix applied: converted train_gmm.py to per-lambda training loop (6 lambdas, 30 epochs each, encoder LR=5e-5, entropy LR=1e-4, early stopping, validation every 5 epochs). Created pipeline/gmm_train.py and pipeline/gmm_eval.py as clean canonical versions. Backup at backup_from_droplet/train_gmm.py also updated.

JEPA training implemented 2026-04-25. Three files were created/updated: (1) src/scripts/train.py — Phase 0 is now JEPA warmup (encoder+pred train with gamma=5, delta=0.1, lambda=0), Phase 1 uses full loss R + lambda*D + gamma*L_JEPA + delta*SIGReg. (2) pipeline/jepa_train.py — canonical per-lambda JEPA+RD training with predictor + context entropy model, loss matches paper eq 5. (3) pipeline/jepa_eval.py — eval with I-frame + P-frame (predictor-based residual coding) + x265 comparison. Batch dimension handling fixed: latents reshaped to [B, T, ...] to keep batch items separate across frames. compute_bdrate.py updated with CLI arg for CSV path. Files in pipeline/ are the canonical clean versions for deployment to droplet.
